mission.title: kpt: kpt doesn't work with symlinks
mission.description: kpt doesn't work with symlinks. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify kpt troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Verify your kpt version:
```bash
kpt version
```
This issue affects kpt when working with symlinked directories. The `kpt live apply` command may prune all resources when invoked on a symlink.

steps.title: Review kpt configuration
steps.description:
Reproduce the symlink issue:
```bash
# Create a package directory and a symlink to it
mkdir -p /tmp/kpt-test/wordpress
ln -s /tmp/kpt-test/wordpress /tmp/kpt-test/wordpress-link

# Apply the package via the real path (works correctly)
kpt live apply /tmp/kpt-test/wordpress

# Apply via symlink (incorrectly prunes all resources)
kpt live apply /tmp/kpt-test/wordpress-link
```
When `kpt live apply` is run on a symlink, it fails to resolve the real path and treats the symlinked directory as empty, causing all previously applied resources to be pruned.

steps.title: Apply the fix for kpt doesn't work with symlinks
steps.description:
The bug causes `kpt live apply` on a symlinked directory to prune all resources because it cannot resolve the symlink to the real package path.

Expected behavior (real path):
```
kpt live apply wordpress
6 resource(s) applied. 0 created, 6 unchanged, 0 configured, 0 failed
```

Buggy behavior (symlink):
```
kpt live apply wordpress-link
deployment.apps/wordpress-mysql pruned
service/wordpress pruned
6 resource(s) pruned, 0 skipped, 0 failed
```

The fix resolves symlinks to their real paths before processing packages.

See the source issue for details: https://github.com/kptdev/kpt/issues/1544

steps.title: Confirm kpt doesn't work with symlinks is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by testing with symlinks:
```bash
# Apply via real path
kpt live apply wordpress

# Apply via symlink - should now work correctly
kpt live apply wordpress-link

# Verify no resources were pruned
kubectl get deployments,services -l app=wordpress
```
Confirm that `kpt live apply` on a symlink behaves identically to the real path.

resolution.summary:
Found a serious bug while working on this issue. If we trigger `kpt live apply` on symlink, it is pruning all resources

```
➜  foo git:(main) ✗ kpt live apply wordpress
deployment.apps/wordpress-mysql unchanged
persistentvolumeclaim/mysql-pv-claim unchanged
persistentvolumeclaim/wp-pv-claim unchanged
service/wordpress unchanged
service/wordpress-mysql unchanged
deployment.apps/wordpress
codeSnippets:
➜  foo git:(main) ✗ kpt live apply wordpress
deployment.apps/wordpress-mysql unchanged
persistentvolumeclaim/mysql-pv-claim unchanged
persistentvolumeclaim/wp-pv-claim unchanged
service/wordpress unchanged
service/wordpress-mysql unchanged
deployment.apps/wordpress unchanged
6 resource(s) applied. 0 created, 6 unchanged, 0 configured, 0 failed
➜  foo git:(main) ✗ kpt live apply wordpress-link
deployment.apps/wordpress-mysql pruned
deployment.apps/wordpress pruned
service/wordpress-mysql pruned
service/wordpress pruned
persistentvolumeclaim/wp-pv-claim pruned
persistentvolumeclaim/mysql-pv-claim pruned
6 resource(s) pruned, 0 skipped, 0 failed

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kpt CLI installed.
